section: Chapter 4: Pressure, Winds, Storms, and Cyclones

questions.question: What is pressure?
questions.explanation: Pressure is defined as force divided by area. When the same force acts on a smaller area, pressure increases.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of pressure?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of pressure is newton per square metre (N/m²), also called pascal.

questions.question: Why do school bags have broad straps?
questions.correct_answer: To reduce pressure on the shoulders
questions.explanation: Broad straps increase the area over which the force acts, reducing pressure.

questions.question: What is atmospheric pressure?
questions.correct_answer: The pressure exerted by the air surrounding the Earth
questions.explanation: Air in the atmosphere exerts pressure on all objects around us.

questions.question: How does liquid pressure change with depth?
questions.explanation: The deeper a point is in a liquid, the greater the pressure exerted by the liquid above it.

questions.question: In which direction do liquids exert pressure?
questions.explanation: Liquids exert pressure on the bottom as well as on the sides of a container.

questions.question: How are winds formed?
questions.correct_answer: By the movement of air from high pressure areas to low pressure areas
questions.explanation: Air moves from regions of high pressure to regions of low pressure, creating wind.

questions.question: What is a thunderstorm?
questions.correct_answer: A storm accompanied by lightning, thunder, and strong winds
questions.explanation: Thunderstorms occur when strong winds cause charge buildup in clouds leading to lightning.

questions.question: What causes lightning?
questions.correct_answer: The sudden discharge of electrical charges between clouds or between clouds and the ground
questions.explanation: When opposite charges build up in clouds, they suddenly discharge producing lightning.

questions.question: What is a cyclone?
questions.correct_answer: A large storm system with strong winds rotating around a low-pressure center
questions.explanation: Cyclones form over warm ocean water due to rising warm air and pressure differences.

notes.title: Pressure
notes.content: Pressure is defined as force acting per unit area.

notes.title: Atmospheric Pressure
notes.content: The air surrounding Earth exerts pressure on all objects. This pressure is called atmospheric pressure.

notes.title: Liquid Pressure
notes.content: Liquids exert pressure in all directions and the pressure increases with depth.

notes.title: Wind Formation
notes.content: Wind is caused by the movement of air from regions of high pressure to low pressure.

notes.title: Cyclones
notes.content: Cyclones are powerful storms that form over warm oceans and involve rotating winds around a low-pressure center.

facts.fact: Pressure increases when the area of contact decreases.
Air exerts pressure in all directions.
Wind always blows from high pressure regions to low pressure regions.
Cyclones form mainly over warm ocean waters.
